posts.author: covfefe77
posts.title: SAT NITE & SUN MORNIN

posts.raw_content:
* **He overthrew governments.** Under Dulles, the CIA helped remove Iran’s prime minister Mohammad Mossadegh in 1953 and Guatemala’s elected president Jacobo Árbenz in 1954, replacing them with regimes friendlier to Washington. ([Office of the Historian][2])
* **He approved human experimentation.** Dulles personally authorized MKULTRA in 1953. CIA projects secretly administered LSD to unwitting people; a Senate investigation described serious rights violations and at least one resulting death. 
* **His CIA considered assassination normal policy work.** The agency plotted against Fidel Castro and prepared an operation to assassinate Congo’s Patrice Lumumba. Evidence does not show that the CIA directly performed Lumumba’s eventual killing, but it had actively explored murdering him. ([National Security Archive][3])
* **He promoted covert war without calling it war.** Propaganda, bribery, sabotage, paramilitary forces and manipulated elections let the United States attack other countries while publicly denying responsibility. ([Office of the Historian][4])
* **He helped create the Bay of Pigs disaster.** His CIA organized and trained the invasion force, while operating on assumptions that proved badly wrong. The invasion collapsed within two days and strengthened Castro. ([Office of the Historian][5])
* **He was insulated from consequences.** Secrecy and compartmentalization meant presidents, Congress and sometimes even senior CIA officials could avoid knowing—or claim they did not know—operational details. Later Senate investigations concluded that this system enabled illegal and unethical conduct. 

The basic lesson: **Dulles transformed the CIA from an intelligence service into a covert political-action machine.** He treated foreign governments and ordinary human beings as pieces on a Cold War chessboard. He was not acting alone—presidents and national-security officials approved much of it—but he was exceptionally effective at making ruthless actions seem like routine paperwork.

[1]: https://www.eisenhowerlibrary.gov/sites/default/files/research/online-documents/aerial-intelligence/key-individuals.pdf?utm_source=chatgpt.com "Eisenhower Presidential Library"
[2]: https://history.state.gov/historicaldocuments/frus1951-54Iran "Historical Documents - Office of the Historian"
[3]: https://nsarchive.gwu.edu/briefing-book/intelligence/2025-11-20/cia-assassination-plots-church-committee-report-50-years?utm_source=chatgpt.com "CIA Assassination Plots: The Church Committee Report 50 Years Later"
[4]: https://history.state.gov/historicaldocuments/frus1961-63v10/d169 "Historical Documents - Office of the Historian"
[5]: https://history.state.gov/milestones/1961-1968/bay-of-pigs "Milestones in the History of U.S. Foreign Relations - Office of the Historian"
posts.author: HeNotMedicated
posts.title: Allen Dulles ran the CIA from 1953 to 1961. His reputation as a “mean motherfucker” comes not from personal violence, but from exercising enormous power secretly, with weak oversight and little concern for people harmed abroad. DEEP STATE DULLES.
posts.content:
• He overthrew governments. Under Dulles, the CIA helped remove Iran’s prime minister Mohammad Mossadegh in 1953 and Guatemala’s elected president Jacobo Árbenz in 1954, replacing them with regimes friendlier to Washington. (Office of the Historian)
  • He approved human experimentation. Dulles personally authorized MKULTRA in 1953. CIA projects secretly administered LSD to unwitting people; a Senate investigation described serious rights violations and at least one resulting death.
  • His CIA considered assassination normal policy work. The agency plotted against Fidel Castro and prepared an operation to assassinate Congo’s Patrice Lumumba. Evidence does not show that the CIA directly performed Lumumba’s eventual killing, but it had actively explored murdering him. (National Security Archive)
  • He promoted covert war without calling it war. Propaganda, bribery, sabotage, paramilitary forces and manipulated elections let the United States attack other countries while publicly denying responsibility. (Office of the Historian)
  • He helped create the Bay of Pigs disaster. His CIA organized and trained the invasion force, while operating on assumptions that proved badly wrong. The invasion collapsed within two days and strengthened Castro. (Office of the Historian)
  • He was insulated from consequences. Secrecy and compartmentalization meant presidents, Congress and sometimes even senior CIA officials could avoid knowing—or claim they did not know—operational details. Later Senate investigations concluded that this system enabled illegal and unethical conduct.

The basic lesson: Dulles transformed the CIA from an intelligence service into a covert political-action machine. He treated foreign governments and ordinary human beings as pieces on a Cold War chessboard. He was not acting alone—presidents and national-security officials approved much of it—but he was exceptionally effective at making ruthless actions seem like routine paperwork.
